decisions.decision: Freeze the reusable cleanup seam as one market-scoped top-level helper in `backend/server.py` that scans `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE`, mirroring `settle_market_account_risk(market_id, timestamp)`, while keeping any per-account mutation helper private to the implementation.
alternatives_rejected: Introduce a separately named public account-scoped exposure cleanup API and make callers orchestrate the market loop themselves.
Duplicate market-removal loops inside each resolve-path caller instead of centralizing the logic in one backend primitive.

decisions.decision: Have the market cleanup helper return a structured per-account removal summary that records the resolved `marketId`, the removed exposure rows, and whether the account was pruned, so `resolve_market_command(...)` and accepted-resolution event assembly can reuse the helper result directly.
alternatives_rejected: Return no value and force later resolve/event code to rescan `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` for test and payload data.
Return only a flat list of affected account ids without enough detail to describe which exposure rows were removed.

decisions.decision: When cleanup removes one market from an account that still has live positions elsewhere, advance the surviving account document's `updatedAt` to the resolution timestamp; if the removed market was the account's final live exposure, prune the entire account document.
alternatives_rejected: Preserve the previous account timestamp from the most recent surviving trade even though resolution mutated the live projection.
Keep empty exposure account documents after cleanup and rely on read-time filtering alone.

decisions.decision: Implement cleanup defensively over seeded or partially normalized exposure state by normalizing the account container and removing rows based on resolved-market identity even when composite keys drift, while keeping the helper scoped to targeted cleanup instead of a general repair pass.
alternatives_rejected: Assume every `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` document is perfectly canonical and let malformed seeded state crash or escape cleanup.
Expand this step into a broad exposure-state migration or repair routine unrelated to resolution removal.

decisions.decision: Wire the new cleanup helper into `resolve_market_command(...)` in this same change and thread its structured summary through the resolution payload so resolved-market exposure disappears from the live model immediately instead of waiting for a later extraction-only follow-up.
alternatives_rejected: Land the cleanup primitive without calling it from market resolution yet.
Mutate exposure during resolution but drop the helper summary instead of exposing one canonical result for tests and accepted-event metadata.

steps.task: Add the new exposure-resolution cleanup seam in `backend/server.py` beside the existing exposure helpers: a small private per-account mutation utility if useful internally, plus the public market-scoped helper that scans `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE`, removes every position row for the resolved `marketId` across all outcomes, advances surviving account timestamps, prunes empty accounts, and returns a deterministic structured removal summary.

Make the cleanup helper tolerant of hand-seeded or partially normalized exposure documents by reusing `ensure_account_exposure_state(...)`-style normalization for account containers, skipping non-dict rows safely, and removing resolved-market rows even when composite keys and stored `marketId` values drift.

Integrate the new helper into the market-resolution path by updating `resolve_market_command(...)` to invoke it alongside `settle_market_account_risk(...)`, storing the returned exposure cleanup summary on the resolution result, and extending the accepted-resolution event builder to emit that summary from the same source of truth.

Add focused backend regressions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` for the helper itself, covering multi-outcome market removal, account `updatedAt` advancement when other markets survive, pruning when the final live position disappears, and tolerance of malformed or drifted seeded exposure state.

Add integration regressions around market resolution to prove resolved-market exposure is removed immediately from live state, unaffected markets still survive, `get_account_exposure(account_id)` keeps its `404` contract for fully pruned accounts, and the accepted resolution payload carries the cleanup summary without regressing existing `assetDelta` assertions.
